close_reason: Gate v1 complete: auth, signing, threshold, server, SQLite persistence, Python+TS clients, vault credential encryption, cross-client vectors. Broker/policy/e-sign deferred to v2.
description:
Goal: move high-risk external API execution (e.g., wires) to a trusted broker model with threshold approval, exactly-once execution, and production-grade auditability.

Scope:
- Canonical intent + permit model
- Durable request/execution state
- Broker integration (no credential injection in gate)
- Policy + recipes model
- Security hardening and ops controls

Out of scope:
- Provider-specific UI polish
- Non-essential SDK ergonomics

Success criteria:
- 3-of-4 approvals can authorize one constrained action
- Action executes exactly once under concurrent triggers
- Secrets are never returned to callers
notes: Gate v1 core COMPLETE: auth, signing, threshold, server, SQLite persistence, comprehensive test suite (spec_test.go). Remaining sub-issues (broker, policy, e-sign) are v2 features that require the TypeScript client first.
title: Gate v1: trusted broker execution for threshold-approved external actions

close_reason: Schemas defined implicitly through GateSignable, ApprovalSignable, GateConversationMessage types. Wire format documented in spec_test.go.
description:
Define deterministic, signed artifacts for threshold authorization and broker execution.

Requirements:
- Canonical hash domain (org_id, request_id, policy_version, action_type)
- Bound constraints: method, host, path, payload hash, max_amount, recipient constraints, expiry, nonce
- Approvals payload binds signer KID and request hash
- ExecutionPermit is one-time and scoped to executor identity
- JSON + CBOR serialization spec with test vectors

Acceptance:
- Cross-package deterministic hashing tests
- Signature verification tests for mixed signer sets
- Expiry/nonce rejection tests
title: Define canonical ExecutionIntent and ExecutionPermit schemas

description:
Integrate a human signature step into the threshold execution pipeline for actions that require wet-sign equivalent e-sign completion.

Requirements:
- New action mode: requires_human_signature=true in ExecutionIntent/policy profile
- Gate/broker creates signature request via provider API and stores envelope/request IDs
- Execution is blocked until provider webhook/status confirms completed signatures
- Signed artifact + audit evidence (timestamp, signer identity, doc hash, envelope ID) stored immutably
- Timeout/decline/cancel paths reflected in gate request status

Provider architecture:
- Provider interface abstraction in broker (DocuSign, Dropbox Sign adapters)
- Start with one default provider; keep API contract provider-agnostic
- Webhook signature verification required

Acceptance:
- Integration test: approved action enters waiting_signature then executes only after signed callback
- Decline/expire test: request transitions to terminal non-executed state
- Evidence record retrievable for audit
notes:
Starting implementation slice: provider interface + Dropbox Sign and DocuSign adapters with webhook verification and tests.
Implementation slice delivered:
- Added provider-agnostic `esign.Provider` interface and request/status models
- Implemented Dropbox Sign adapter (template send, status poll, webhook verification)
- Implemented DocuSign adapter (template envelope create, status poll, webhook verification)
- Added adapter tests and docs (`docs/e-sign-providers.md`)

Remaining for this bead:
- Wire provider calls into broker/gate request lifecycle with waiting_signature state
- Persist signature evidence and webhook-driven transitions in durable store
title: Human e-sign integration for approval-required actions

description:
Integrate the new `esign` package with broker/gate flow.

Requirements:
- Add `waiting_signature` lifecycle state
- Trigger provider `CreateSignatureRequest` after multisig approval for signature-required actions
- Consume webhook/status updates to transition to executed/declined/expired
- Persist provider request IDs and signature evidence for audit

Acceptance:
- Integration test covers approved->waiting_signature->executed path
- Integration test covers declined/expired terminal states
title: Wire e-sign providers into broker execution lifecycle

close_reason: Implemented: SQLiteStore with durable state, gate.executed marker prevents re-execution, idempotency test passes
description:
Replace in-memory request/execution state with durable storage and exactly-once semantics.

Requirements:
- Persist org config, requests, approvals, and executed markers
- Atomic compare-and-set lock on (org_id, request_id) before outbound execution
- Crash-safe recovery: in-flight states resolve deterministically
- Replay resistance across restart

Acceptance:
- Concurrent execute test proves one downstream execution
- Restart test proves duplicate execute is blocked
- No regression to threshold/expiry behavior
title: Implement durable gate state and atomic single-use execution lock

close_reason: Implemented: admin token auth, signature verification on all messages, credential scrubbing, audit logging
description:
Harden gate surface so sensitive state and execution paths require explicit authn/authz.

Requirements:
- Remove credential leakage from org read APIs
- Separate admin config auth from execution auth
- Add role model: config-admin, requester, approver, executor
- Require authenticated context on execute/scan in production mode

Acceptance:
- Unauthorized access tests for every sensitive endpoint
- Existing dev mode remains explicit and noisy
title: Enforce gate API auth boundaries and least privilege

description:
Gate should stop constructing authenticated upstream requests directly.

Requirements:
- Gate emits validated ExecutionPermit to broker
- Gate passes idempotency key and correlation metadata
- Gate receives broker execution receipt and persists result
- mTLS or signed service-token auth between gate and broker

Acceptance:
- Integration test with fake broker verifies permit contract
- Gate never loads provider API key material
title: Add broker integration API to gate (permit-based, no credential injection)

description:
Implement broker service that performs constrained execution while keeping secrets non-exportable.

Requirements:
- Integrate Vault/KMS/secret manager backend
- No endpoint that returns raw credential values
- Credential templates support header/token injection at execution time
- Strict egress policy by service profile and path template

Acceptance:
- Broker e2e tests show action succeeds without key exposure
- Secret rotation test passes without gate change
title: Build trusted broker executor with secret-store-backed credentials

description:
Design policy control so multisig has final authority while defaults remain safe.

Direction:
- Ship baseline endpoint profiles with qntm (service, host allowlist, method/path templates, risk tier)
- Allow org-specific overlay policies approved by multisig (e.g., 3-of-4)
- Recipes reference approved profiles and parameter schemas
- Any off-profile endpoint requires explicit multisig policy grant

Acceptance:
- Policy resolution order is deterministic and documented
- Recipe execution fails closed for unapproved endpoints
- Governance update flow for policy overlays is tested
title: Policy model: shipped endpoint profiles + multisig-owned overlays + recipes

description:
Add production ops controls and forensic traceability.

Requirements:
- Append-only audit log with request/permit/execution IDs
- Reconciliation worker to detect provider pending/failed divergence
- Alerting hooks for failed/late/high-risk executions
- Break-glass runbook + emergency disable switch

Acceptance:
- Simulated failure/retry scenarios produce coherent audit trail
- Ops docs include on-call and incident response flow
title: Audit, reconciliation, and break-glass operations for broker executions

close_reason: Implemented: 918 lines of tests covering replay, signatures, expiration, duplicates, idempotency, threshold enforcement
description:
Expand tests for adversarial and concurrency behavior before production usage.

Requirements:
- Parallel execute race test (N concurrent triggers)
- Replay attempts across process restarts
- Policy bypass attempts (host/path/method mismatch)
- Permit tampering and stale-expiry fuzz cases

Acceptance:
- Tests fail on current vulnerable patterns and pass with fixes
- CI includes this suite in release gates
title: Hardening test suite: race, replay, and policy bypass

description:
Create a curated default catalog so teams can start safely without writing policy from scratch.

Requirements:
- Bundle signed baseline profiles (service/host/path/method constraints + risk tier)
- Attach recommended default thresholds per profile (e.g., low/med/high risk)
- Include sample recipes that compile only against approved profiles
- Document how multisig can add/override/disable profiles via overlay policy

Acceptance:
- New org can execute at least one low-risk action via shipped recipe
- Off-catalog endpoint fails closed until policy overlay is approved
title: Ship starter endpoint profile catalog and recipe pack

close_reason: TTL-based secret expiry implemented: StoreWithTTL, expiry checking in Get, PurgeExpired, and ExpiresAt field in vault JSON. Tests passing.
description: Secrets provisioned via gate.secret should have configurable TTLs (15min, 60min, 4hr) based on data classification/risk tier. After TTL expires, gateway purges decrypted secret from memory and vault. Requires human to re-provision. This limits prompt injection attack surface from 'always' to a bounded window. gate.secret message should include a ttl field. ConversationVault.Store should track expiry. Gateway polling loop should check and purge expired secrets.
title: Gateway vault: TTL-based secret expiry

close_reason: Human-controlled token refresh: gate.expired message type with ExpiredPayload, checkExpiredCredentials in gateway loop, expiryNotified dedup. Gateway can USE but not CREATE/REFRESH secrets.
description: Gateway should NOT be able to autonomously refresh expired vault tokens. When a secret TTL expires, gateway should post a gate.expired notification to the conversation asking a human to re-provision. This keeps humans in the loop for security-sensitive operations. The gateway is deliberately limited - it can USE secrets but not CREATE or REFRESH them.
title: Gateway vault: human-controlled token refresh

description: No way to search conversations or messages. As conversation count grows this becomes unusable. Need: search bar at top of conversation list, filter conversations by name, search within message history (Cmd/Ctrl+F equivalent), highlight matching results, recent/pinned conversations.
title: Add conversation search and filtering

close_reason: Closed
description: group create fails with: json: unsupported type: map[types.KeyID]*group.GroupMemberInfo. The Members map in GroupState uses types.KeyID as map key which doesn't implement encoding.TextMarshaler. Needs design decision: either implement TextMarshaler on KeyID or change to string-keyed map. Reproduced via: qntm group create 'Test Group' 'desc' → Error: failed to save group state
title: Group messaging JSON serialization bug

close_reason: Closed
description: The AIM UI currently has a local Express server (server/index.mjs) that stores identity keys on disk and does all cryptography. This is wrong - the browser should import @qntm/client directly, do crypto in-browser, and store keys/state in localStorage. The Express server should be completely removed. This massively reduces security footprint by making AIM a static site that only talks to the remote dropbox.
title: Rearchitect AIM UI as static site - eliminate Express server

close_reason: Profile=account, Identity=keypair. Sidebar renamed, status messages updated

close_reason: Fixed gate.request payload hashing and payload field alignment in the AIM UI; added regression coverage.
description: In ui/aim-chat/src/qntm.ts, gateRunRequest hashes JSON.stringify(requestBody) before calling computePayloadHash and serializes request_body instead of the shared payload field. gateApproveRequest later hashes reqMsg.payload, so any request with a body can produce signatures/request hashes that do not match the Python client or gateway. Add regression tests for body-bearing recipes and approval hashing.
title: Fix AIM UI gate.request payload hashing/schema mismatch

description: Gate authorization messages flow through qntm conversations as body_types (gate.request, gate.approval, gate.executed). close_reason: Plaintext leakage audit complete: Scrub() zeroes values, vault encrypts at rest with AES-256-GCM, gateway checks expiry before use.
description: Audit and harden: (1) Gateway must never log secret values. (2) gate.executed/gate.result messages must not include credential values. (3) Secrets must not appear in conversation history or memory. (4) ConversationVault should zero memory after use (best effort in GC languages). (5) Vault key should not be stored alongside encrypted data. Inspired by OpenClaw vault hardening practices.
title: Gateway vault: prevent plaintext secret leakage

description: Rename confusing labels across both UIs to plain, user-friendly alternatives. Key renames: 'Create + self-join' → 'New Conversation'. 'Check mail' → 'Refresh' or 'Check for Messages'. 'Promote to Gate' → 'Enable API Gateway'. 'Provision secret' → 'Add API Key'. 'dropbox' → 'message relay' or 'relay server' (avoids Dropbox trademark confusion). 'Threshold' → 'Required Approvals'. 'Recipe' → 'API Template'. 'KID' → 'Key ID' (spell it out in UI, abbreviate only in technical contexts). Every label should be understandable without reading docs.
title: Rename confusing UI labels and actions

description: Gateway becomes a qntm conversation participant. Promoting a chat = inviting a gateway. Secrets sent encrypted to gateway via conversation. Recipes need arguments. Users can use qntm default gateway or deploy their own.
design:
# Gateway as Conversation Participant

## Core Concept
Gateway is a qntm identity with its own Ed25519 keypair. It joins conversations as a participant and listens for gate messages by polling its dropbox — same as any user. The conversation IS the state. No separate org store, no dual-write.

## Key Changes from Current Design
- **Org goes away**. Conversation participants = signers. Threshold rules set via gate.config messages.
- **No HTTP message path**. Gateway polls dropbox, reads encrypted messages. Eliminates dual-write problem.
- **Secrets encrypted to gateway**. NaCl box (X25519) sealed to gateway's public key. Only gateway can decrypt. Other participants see the secret was provisioned but can't read the value.
- **Recipes have arguments**. path_params, query_params, body_schema. Client resolves templates before signing. What you approve is exactly what executes.
- **Pluggable**. Use qntm's default gateway or deploy your own. Just needs a keypair and access to the dropbox.

## New Message Types
- gate.promote — marks conversation as gate-enabled, sets initial threshold rules
- gate.secret — encrypted credential for gateway's vault
- gate.config — update threshold rules
- gate.request — (existing) now includes arguments map and recipe_name
- gate.approval — (existing) unchanged
- gate.executed — (existing) unchanged
- gate.result — API response body posted back to conversation

## Flows
1. **Promote**: Create conversation → invite gateway → send gate.promote with threshold rules
2. **Secret**: Encrypt credential to gateway pubkey → send gate.secret → gateway decrypts and vaults
3. **Request**: Pick recipe → fill arguments → client resolves templates → sign resolved URL → send gate.request
4. **Approve**: See full request with arguments/URL → click Approve → threshold met → gateway executes
5. **Execute**: Gateway sees threshold met → looks up credential in vault → makes API call → posts gate.executed + gate.result

## Signing
GateSignable replaces org_id with conversation_id. Signature covers resolved URL (not template), so approvers sign exactly what executes.

## Phases
Phase 1: Recipe arguments (extend Recipe struct, update starter.json, UI argument form)
Phase 2: Gateway identity + message loop (new gateway.go, polls dropbox, processes gate messages)
Phase 3: Promote flow (gate.promote message, conversation → gate-enabled)
Phase 4: Secret provisioning (NaCl box encryption, per-conversation vault)
Phase 5: Pluggable gateways (gateway init, gateway serve, documentation)
notes:
Additional design constraints from live testing (2026-03-14):

1. PROMOTION REQUIRES GATEWAY PUBKEY: When promoting, the UI should request a pubkey from the gateway. Receiving the pubkey back is a prerequisite for promotion to complete. This ensures the gateway is reachable and ready before the conversation becomes gate-enabled.

2. GATEWAY KEY CANNOT APPROVE: The gateway's key must be excluded from the approval set. It can read messages, decrypt secrets, execute API calls, and post results — but it cannot count toward the approval threshold. The m-of-n threshold is strictly human signers.

3. ONE KEY PER CONVERSATION: On the gateway side, each conversation gets its own isolated keypair. This limits blast radius — compromising one conversation's gateway key doesn't affect others. Implementation detail left to gateway operator.

4. FLOW REVISION: Promote flow becomes:
   a. User initiates promotion in UI
   b. UI contacts gateway (via dropbox or direct) requesting a conversation-specific pubkey
   c. Gateway generates keypair, stores it, returns pubkey
   d. UI adds gateway as conversation participant using the returned pubkey
   e. UI sends gate.promote message with threshold rules (gateway excluded from signer count)
   f. Gateway begins polling the conversation for gate messages
title: Rearchitect gate: gateway as conversation participant

close_reason: Did-you-mean suggestions, post-action hints, consistent usage messages
description: TUI /help dumps a wall of text. Each command needs its own help. Need: /help <command> for per-command help (e.g., /help invite shows what invites are, args, examples). Tab completion should show brief description next to each command. After executing a command, show next-step hint (e.g., after /invite, say 'Share this token with your contact. They can join with /join <token>'). Error messages should suggest the correct command when user makes a mistake.
title: Add contextual help to TUI slash commands

close_reason: NaCl box encryption (Go + JS), per-conversation vault with AES-256-GCM, gateway handleSecret, CLI gate-secret command, UI secret provisioning, 40+ tests passing
description: Encrypt API credentials to gateway public key using NaCl box (Ed25519→X25519 conversion). gate.secret message contains encrypted_blob, service, header_name, header_template. Gateway decrypts and stores in per-conversation vault (filesystem or SQLite, AES-256-GCM at rest). CLI: qntm gate secret --conv <id> --service <name>. UI: Add Secret modal with service/header/value fields.
title: Secret provisioning via gate.secret messages

close_reason: Feature-complete Python QSP client library with 28 passing tests. All cross-client vectors match Go/TS. Implements: QSP1Suite (Ed25519, HKDF-SHA256, XChaCha20-Poly1305, X25519), identity, invite, message create/decrypt, key wrapping, CBOR encoding. Dependencies: PyNaCl, cryptography, cbor2.
description: Implement a native Python QSP client (no Go dependency). Must implement QSP1Suite: Ed25519 keygen/sign/verify, HKDF-SHA256 key derivation, XChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD, X25519 conversions, canonical CBOR, deterministic nonces. Plus message create/decrypt, invite create/parse/join, conversation management. Must pass existing cross-client test vectors. Use pynacl/cryptography + cbor2. This replaces the current Go binary wrapper in python-dist/.
title: Feature-complete Python QSP client library

Update starter.json recipes with param definitions (e.g. hn.get-item needs id path param, leet.translate needs text body param). Update GateConversationMessage to include arguments map and recipe_name.
title: Recipe arguments: extend Recipe struct with params and body schema
